question: In Next.js App Router, what file name creates a page that is visible in the browser?
options: index.tsx
page.tsx
route.tsx
view.tsx

question: What URL does the file app/tracks/[track]/page.tsx handle?
options: Only /tracks/typescript — dynamic routes need explicit files
Any URL matching /tracks/<anything>
/tracks only
It handles all URLs in the app

question: In Next.js 15, how do you read dynamic route params in a page component?
options: const { track } = params — params is a plain object
const { track } = useParams() — it is a React hook
const { track } = await params — params is a Promise in Next.js 15
import params from 'next/params'

question: What is the purpose of layout.tsx?
options: It defines the URL for a route
It wraps all pages in its folder with a shared UI shell (header, sidebar etc.)
It handles API requests for a route
It is the same as page.tsx but for static content

question: Which component type should you use by default in the App Router, and when do you add 'use client'?
options: Client Components by default — add 'use server' for server logic
Server Components by default — add 'use client' only when the component needs interactivity or browser APIs
Always use Client Components for correctness
It depends on the Next.js version
